commands.hypothesis: price-path-v2 cohort B headline finding: tokens with ≤3 snipers in T+0..T+2 had ~100% top-quartile winner rate. Isolating as standalone filter at the wider ≤3 threshold should net ≥1.5 SOL on n≥100 with drop_top3>0. TP/SL=50/30 from best-combos empirical consensus (SL=30 picked in 10/12 high-n combos).

price-path-v2 found a 2D pattern: high whale share of buy SOL (d=+0.25) AND few unique buyers (d=-0.29) both correlate with realized PnL. Translates to 'a few large conviction buyers, not many small bundler bots.' Novel — no current strategy captures this. Coverage gap: buy_pressure_* is new-only, so only fires on recent grads.

price-path-v2 cohort A's strongest non-tautological signals: monotonicity_0_30 d=+1.54 (87.7% top-Q WR) and max_drawdown_0_30 d=+0.36. Peak-analysis: mono>0.66 has p75_peak=54.2, dd>-10% has p75=53.3 and the best avg_final (+4.8%) of any single filter — TP=50 captures the p75 winners cleanly.

price-path-v2 cross-cohort robust signals: top10_wallet_pct negative d in BOTH cohorts (-0.25 / -0.26), flow_imbalance_t30 positive in BOTH (+0.48 / +0.24). Mirrors best-combo 'top10 < 25% + confirmed_recovery' (TP=50/SL=30, +14.22% on n=38) but uses the flow signal instead of confirmed_recovery — should fire more often.

Revised from initial 'small init_lp wins' v2 finding after cross-checking best-combos.json. init_lp > 15 is one of the top empirical performers (TP=50/SL=30, +10.42% on n=82 with confirmed_recovery). The v2 negative-d signal was at the EXTREME-low end (true rugs); the meaningful signal is init_lp >= 15. Pairing with flow_imbalance to avoid catching dying markets.

Triple-stack of the three cross-cohort-confirmed price-path-v2 signals (sniper count negative, top10 wallet negative, flow imbalance positive). Wider thresholds than single-filter strategies (≤5 / <30 / >0) because AND-stacking already cuts volume. TP=75 instead of 50 because triple-quality entries justify a wider target. SL=30 stays — same empirical consensus.
